body_txt: Actors are not expected to be exactly like the character they are playing, but there is generally an expectation that the actor will reflect something about the character. Irony As She Is Cast is when there are specific traits associated with a character and the actor is something entirely opposite of that. A classically trained opera singer plays someone who cannot carry a tune to save their life . A married couple play characters who have strong, mutual loathing . A Master Actor portrays someone with zero interest in the theater . A former model is put into a fatsuit with a shaggy wig and discolored make-up . In essence, this is the opposite effect of Meta Casting . Casting a talented actor as an untalented character is actually more complicated than it may appear, as it usually requires a great deal of precision. You have to be off-tune in exactly the right way to be clearly off-tune , at exactly the right time to engage maximum laughter; the level of skill involved to be believably bad is frequently much greater than the skill needed to perform "properly". While their instincts may still kick in , it's generally easier for them to tune down their capabilities than it is for a novice to know what to look for. Thus you still end up using The Cast Show Off but only a few can catch what was done. This version often implements Dreadful Musician , Hollywood Tone-Deaf , or Bad "Bad Acting" (or any of the other tropes under Stylistic Suck ). It can be seen as many actors are encouraged to exercise with regularity , even with some martial arts involved, but have to play a wimpy dork despite having obvious muscle tone . Some works aim for this from the front, if anything for the notoriety of an actor defying a Typecasting . Actors may enjoy it playing with a career defining role , and demonstrate a sense of humor about themselves while still stretching their performance muscles. Or The Powers That Be simply thought it would be funny . Irony as She Is Cast might be reactionary in a long-lived role; extreme cases might try to counter what's Ripped from the Headlines . Generally not applicable to the likes of Dawson Casting or Fake Nationality , unless the specific character traits are exaggerated as much as possible such as Captain Patriotic . A supertrope of Jews Playing Nazis . Compare Casting Gag , when an actor is cast in a role that mirrors or parodies a previous role or experience, and Playing Against Type , where the actor plays a character contrary to the previous characters. Contrast Cast the Expert where this person was chosen specifically for their talent, Ability Over Appearance when the performance matters more than the appearance of the actor, The Cast Showoff where the talent gets to be in the limelight and Actor-Shared Background where an actor's characteristics line up with that of their character's. The Trope Namer is English as She Is Spoke , probably the first real-life example of My Hovercraft Is Full of Eels , and not really related to the trope at all. But it makes for a good trope name.
